name: Hugh Masekela
abstract: Hugh Ramapolo Masekela (4 April 1939 – 23 January 2018) was a South African trumpeter, flugelhornist, cornetist, singer and composer who was described as "the father of South African jazz". Masekela was known for his jazz compositions and for writing well-known anti-apartheid songs such as "Soweto Blues" and "Bring Him Back Home".

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: When we went to the French Riviera for the first time, they had a new guy from South Africa. That was Hugh Masekela.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Hugh Masekela played there, but that was about the – they had – but it was more like Sissy Houston.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Cobb — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Darius Brubeck
about_quotes.evidence: When Victor came to the States... he hooked up with Hugh Masekela who was living in the Bronx at the time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Darius Brubeck interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 29, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Putter Smith
about_quotes.evidence: one of my very favorite bass players and people is Henry Franklin who was Hugh Masekela’s bass player.
about_quotes.doc_title: Putter Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 27, 2024
